================================================================================

DESCRIPTION:
This table provides a complete view of customer conversations across both Intercom 
(current system) and Hiver (legacy system used until April 2025). When we migrated 
from Hiver to Intercom, conversations were imported but lost critical metadata. 
This view reconstructs the complete picture by:
- Excluding Hiver-imported conversations from Intercom (tagged 'ignore-for-reporting')
- Including original Hiver data with full metadata preserved
- Providing unified conversation-level metrics across both platforms

USE CASES:
- Historical reporting spanning both support platforms
- Customer service metrics and KPIs
- Response time and resolution analysis
- CSAT tracking across the platform transition
- Team and admin performance metrics

KEY COLUMNS:
- id: Conversation identifier
- created_at: When the conversation started
- inbox_name: Team/inbox assignment
- admin_name: Assigned support agent
- time_to_admin_reply: Response time in seconds
- time_to_first_close: Resolution time in seconds
- is_closed: Whether conversation is resolved
- was_auto_closed: If closed by automation (bot ID: 6649497)
- conversation_length: Total number of messages
- conversation_admin_length: Number of admin messages
- conversation_user_length: Number of customer messages
- rating: CSAT score (1=Not satisfied, 3=Okay, 5=Satisfied)
- tag_1 through tag_6: Conversation tags
- original_source: Source platform ('Intercom' or 'Hiver')
- customer_email: Email address of the customer (from first contact for Intercom, mailbox_contact for Hiver)
- org_id: Organization ID using two-tier lookup:
  1. Exact email match in mysql_users (highest priority)
  2. Domain-based match for company emails when exact match fails (excludes generic providers)

DATA SOURCES:
- public.intercom_conversations: Current support platform (April 2025 - present)
- public.intercom_conversation_parts: Message details for length calculations
- public.intercom_contacts: Contact email information
- public.hiver_new_conversations: Legacy platform data (through April 2025)
- public.intercom_teams: Team/inbox information
- public.intercom_admins: Support agent information
- public.mysql_users: User information for org_id lookup
- public.mysql_organizations: Organization details

DEDUPLICATION STRATEGY:
Hiver conversations were imported into Intercom in April 2025 but tagged with 
'ignore-for-reporting' to prevent double-counting. This view:
1. Filters OUT imported Hiver data from Intercom side
2. Includes original Hiver data with complete metadata
3. Uses UNION to combine both sources without duplicates

FILTERING LOGIC:
- Excludes conversations with custom attribute "Ticket category" (likely test/internal)
- Excludes any conversation tagged 'ignore-for-reporting' from Intercom
- Uses row_number() to deduplicate when conversations have multiple parts

PERFORMANCE CONSIDERATIONS:
- Distributed by conversation ID for optimal join performance
- Sorted by creation date for time-series queries
- Table format (not view) to avoid CASCADE issues with materialized views

USAGE EXAMPLES:
-- Get daily conversation metrics
SELECT 
  DATE(created_at) as date,
  COUNT(*) as total_conversations,
  AVG(time_to_admin_reply) as avg_response_time,
  AVG(CASE WHEN rating IS NOT NULL THEN rating END) as avg_csat
FROM view_cx_conversations
GROUP BY 1
ORDER BY 1 DESC;

-- Analyze inbox workload
SELECT 
  inbox_name,
  COUNT(*) as conversation_count,
  AVG(conversation_length) as avg_length,
  SUM(CASE WHEN is_closed THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)::FLOAT / COUNT(*) as close_rate
FROM view_cx_conversations
GROUP BY 1;

RELATED OBJECTS:
- view_intercom_conversations_with_topics: Message-level topic analysis
- Metabase dashboards: Primary consumer for support metrics

LAST MODIFIED: 2025-02-20
OWNER: Data Team
================================================================================
*/

-- Step 1: Drop the _new table if it exists (cleanup from any previous failed runs)
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS view_cx_conversations_new;

-- Step 2: Create the new table with fresh data
CREATE TABLE view_cx_conversations_new
DISTKEY(id)
SORTKEY(created_at)
AS
WITH domain_to_org AS (
  -- Map email domains to org_ids, excluding generic email providers
  -- Only include domains that map to exactly one org for accuracy
  SELECT 
    LOWER(SPLIT_PART(email, '@', 2)) as domain,
    organizationid as org_id,
    COUNT(*) as user_count
  FROM public.mysql_users
  WHERE email IS NOT NULL 
    AND email LIKE '%@%'
    AND organizationid IS NOT NULL
    -- Exclude generic email providers
    AND LOWER(SPLIT_PART(email, '@', 2)) NOT IN (
      'gmail.com', 'outlook.com', 'icloud.com', 'hotmail.com', 'yahoo.com',
      'proton.me', 'protonmail.com', 'me.com', 'pm.me', 'live.com',
      'qq.com', 'aol.com', '163.com', 'fastmail.com', 'mail.com',
      'yandex.ru', 'mail.ru', 'gmx.com', 'gmx.net', 'web.de',
      'tutanota.com', 'duck.com', 'msn.com', 'comcast.net', 'verizon.net'
    )
  GROUP BY 1, 2
  -- Use the domain with the most users if there are multiple orgs
  QUALIFY ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY domain ORDER BY user_count DESC) = 1
),
intercom_conversations_filtered AS (
  SELECT
    ic.id,
    TIMESTAMP 'epoch' + ic.created_at * INTERVAL '1 second' AS created_at,
    it.name AS inbox_name, 
    ia.name AS admin_name, 
    ic.statistics.time_to_admin_reply::INTEGER AS time_to_admin_reply,
    ic.statistics.time_to_first_close::INTEGER AS time_to_first_close,
    ic.state = 'closed' AS is_closed,
    -- Bot user ID 6649497 indicates auto-closed
    COALESCE(ic.statistics.last_closed_by_id::VARCHAR = '6649497', FALSE) AS was_auto_closed,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY ic.id) AS rn,
    -- Calculate conversation length including initial message
    (LEAST(1, SUM(CASE WHEN cp.part_type = 'assignment' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) OVER (PARTITION BY ic.id)) +
     SUM(CASE WHEN cp.part_type = 'comment' AND cp.author.type IN ('admin', 'user') THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) OVER (PARTITION BY ic.id) + 1)::FLOAT AS conversation_length,
    -- Admin message count
    (LEAST(1, SUM(CASE WHEN cp.part_type = 'assignment' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) OVER (PARTITION BY ic.id)) +
     SUM(CASE WHEN cp.part_type = 'comment' AND cp.author.type = 'admin' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) OVER (PARTITION BY ic.id))::FLOAT AS conversation_admin_length,
    -- User message count (including initial)
    (SUM(CASE WHEN cp.part_type = 'comment' AND cp.author.type = 'user' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) OVER (PARTITION BY ic.id) + 1)::FLOAT AS conversation_user_length,
    ic.conversation_rating.rating::INTEGER AS rating,
    -- Extract up to 6 tags
    ic.tags.tags[0].name::TEXT AS tag_1,
    ic.tags.tags[1].name::TEXT AS tag_2,
    ic.tags.tags[2].name::TEXT AS tag_3,
    ic.tags.tags[3].name::TEXT AS tag_4,
    ic.tags.tags[4].name::TEXT AS tag_5,
    ic.tags.tags[5].name::TEXT AS tag_6,
    -- Get email from first contact in the conversation
    cont.email AS customer_email,
    -- Get org_id from mysql_users (exact match) or domain mapping (fallback)
    COALESCE(
      mu.organizationid,  -- First try exact email match
      dto.org_id          -- Then try domain match
    ) AS org_id,
    -- Get stripe_id via org_id lookup
    mo.stripeid AS stripe_id
  FROM public.intercom_conversations ic
  LEFT JOIN public.intercom_teams it ON it.id = ic.team_assignee_id
  LEFT JOIN public.intercom_admins ia ON ia.id = ic.admin_assignee_id
  LEFT JOIN public.intercom_conversation_parts cp ON cp.conversation_id = ic.id
  -- Join to get email from first contact
  LEFT JOIN public.intercom_contacts cont ON cont.id = ic.contacts.contacts[0].id
  -- Join to mysql_users for exact email match
  LEFT JOIN public.mysql_users mu ON LOWER(mu.email) = LOWER(cont.email)
  -- Fallback join to domain mapping for company emails
  LEFT JOIN domain_to_org dto ON LOWER(SPLIT_PART(cont.email, '@', 2)) = dto.domain
  -- Join to get stripe customer ID
  LEFT JOIN public.mysql_organizations mo ON mo.id = COALESCE(mu.organizationid, dto.org_id)
  WHERE ic.custom_attributes.["Ticket category"] IS NULL
    -- Critical: Exclude imported Hiver conversations to avoid duplicates
    AND (ic.tags.tags[0].name::TEXT IS NULL OR ic.tags.tags[0].name::TEXT <> 'ignore-for-reporting')
    AND (ic.tags.tags[1].name::TEXT IS NULL OR ic.tags.tags[1].name::TEXT <> 'ignore-for-reporting')
    AND (ic.tags.tags[2].name::TEXT IS NULL OR ic.tags.tags[2].name::TEXT <> 'ignore-for-reporting')
    AND (ic.tags.tags[3].name::TEXT IS NULL OR ic.tags.tags[3].name::TEXT <> 'ignore-for-reporting')
    AND (ic.tags.tags[4].name::TEXT IS NULL OR ic.tags.tags[4].name::TEXT <> 'ignore-for-reporting')
    AND (ic.tags.tags[5].name::TEXT IS NULL OR ic.tags.tags[5].name::TEXT <> 'ignore-for-reporting')
)
-- Get Intercom conversations (excluding imported Hiver data)
SELECT 
  id::VARCHAR,
  created_at,
  inbox_name,
  admin_name,
  time_to_admin_reply,
  time_to_first_close,
  is_closed,
  was_auto_closed,
  conversation_length,
  conversation_admin_length,
  conversation_user_length,
  rating,
  tag_1,
  tag_2,
  tag_3,
  tag_4,
  tag_5,
  tag_6,
  'Intercom' AS original_source,
  customer_email,
  org_id
FROM intercom_conversations_filtered
WHERE rn = 1

UNION ALL

-- Add original Hiver conversations with preserved metadata
SELECT
  h.conversation_id::VARCHAR AS id,
  h.created_at::TIMESTAMP,
  CASE 
    WHEN h._ab_source_file_url = '245420_hiver/Anna_Rhatigan_l184d_new.csv' THEN 'Anna'
    WHEN h._ab_source_file_url = '191527_hiver/Priority_l184d_new.csv' THEN 'Priority'
    ELSE SPLIT_PART(SPLIT_PART(h._ab_source_file_url, '/', 2), '_', 1)
  END AS inbox_name,
  CASE WHEN h.first_responder = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE h.first_responder END AS admin_name, 
  CASE WHEN h.first_response_time__business_hours___seconds_ = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE h.first_response_time__business_hours___seconds_::FLOAT END AS time_to_admin_reply,
  CASE WHEN h.resolution_time__business_hours___seconds_ = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE h.resolution_time__business_hours___seconds_::FLOAT END AS time_to_first_close,
  CASE WHEN (h.resolved_at <> 'NA' AND h.resolved_at IS NOT NULL) OR (h.status = 'Closed' AND h.conversation_type <> 'Outbound') THEN TRUE ELSE FALSE END AS is_closed,
  FALSE AS was_auto_closed,
  h.total_emails_count::FLOAT AS conversation_length,
  h.emails_sent_count::FLOAT AS conversation_admin_length,
  h.total_emails_count::FLOAT - h.emails_sent_count::FLOAT AS conversation_user_length,
  CASE 
    WHEN h.csat_rating = 'Not satisfied' THEN 1 
    WHEN h.csat_rating = 'Okay' THEN 3 
    WHEN h.csat_rating = 'Satisfied' THEN 5 
    ELSE NULL 
  END AS rating,
  CASE WHEN h.tags = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE SPLIT_PART(h.tags, ',', 1) END AS tag_1,
  CASE WHEN h.tags = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE SPLIT_PART(h.tags, ',', 2) END AS tag_2,
  CASE WHEN h.tags = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE SPLIT_PART(h.tags, ',', 3) END AS tag_3,
  CASE WHEN h.tags = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE SPLIT_PART(h.tags, ',', 4) END AS tag_4,
  CASE WHEN h.tags = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE SPLIT_PART(h.tags, ',', 5) END AS tag_5,
  CASE WHEN h.tags = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE SPLIT_PART(h.tags, ',', 6) END AS tag_6,
  'Hiver' AS original_source,
  CASE WHEN h.mailbox_contact = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE h.mailbox_contact END AS customer_email,
  -- Get org_id from mysql_users (exact match) or domain mapping (fallback)
  COALESCE(
    mu.organizationid,  -- First try exact email match
    dto.org_id          -- Then try domain match
  ) AS org_id
FROM public.hiver_new_conversations h
-- Join to mysql_users for exact email match
LEFT JOIN public.mysql_users mu ON LOWER(mu.email) = LOWER(CASE WHEN h.mailbox_contact = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE h.mailbox_contact END)
-- Fallback join to domain mapping for company emails
LEFT JOIN domain_to_org dto ON LOWER(SPLIT_PART(CASE WHEN h.mailbox_contact = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE h.mailbox_contact END, '@', 2)) = dto.domain;

-- Step 3: Atomic swap - drop old and rename new
BEGIN;

DROP TABLE IF EXISTS view_cx_conversations;
ALTER TABLE view_cx_conversations_new RENAME TO view_cx_conversations;

COMMIT;

toolUseResult.originalFile:
/*
================================================================================
TABLE: view_cx_conversations
PURPOSE: Unified view of all customer conversations from both Intercom and Hiver
================================================================================

DESCRIPTION:
This table provides a complete view of customer conversations across both Intercom 
(current system) and Hiver (legacy system used until April 2025). When we migrated 
from Hiver to Intercom, conversations were imported but lost critical metadata. 
This view reconstructs the complete picture by:
- Excluding Hiver-imported conversations from Intercom (tagged 'ignore-for-reporting')
- Including original Hiver data with full metadata preserved
- Providing unified conversation-level metrics across both platforms

USE CASES:
- Historical reporting spanning both support platforms
- Customer service metrics and KPIs
- Response time and resolution analysis
- CSAT tracking across the platform transition
- Team and admin performance metrics

KEY COLUMNS:
- id: Conversation identifier
- created_at: When the conversation started
- inbox_name: Team/inbox assignment
- admin_name: Assigned support agent
- time_to_admin_reply: Response time in seconds
- time_to_first_close: Resolution time in seconds
- is_closed: Whether conversation is resolved
- was_auto_closed: If closed by automation (bot ID: 6649497)
- conversation_length: Total number of messages
- conversation_admin_length: Number of admin messages
- conversation_user_length: Number of customer messages
- rating: CSAT score (1=Not satisfied, 3=Okay, 5=Satisfied)
- tag_1 through tag_6: Conversation tags
- original_source: Source platform ('Intercom' or 'Hiver')
- customer_email: Email address of the customer (from first contact for Intercom, mailbox_contact for Hiver)
- org_id: Organization ID using two-tier lookup:
  1. Exact email match in mysql_users (highest priority)
  2. Domain-based match for company emails when exact match fails (excludes generic providers)

DATA SOURCES:
- public.intercom_conversations: Current support platform (April 2025 - present)
- public.intercom_conversation_parts: Message details for length calculations
- public.intercom_contacts: Contact email information
- public.hiver_new_conversations: Legacy platform data (through April 2025)
- public.intercom_teams: Team/inbox information
- public.intercom_admins: Support agent information
- public.mysql_users: User information for org_id lookup
- public.mysql_organizations: Organization details

DEDUPLICATION STRATEGY:
Hiver conversations were imported into Intercom in April 2025 but tagged with 
'ignore-for-reporting' to prevent double-counting. This view:
1. Filters OUT imported Hiver data from Intercom side
2. Includes original Hiver data with complete metadata
3. Uses UNION to combine both sources without duplicates

FILTERING LOGIC:
- Excludes conversations with custom attribute "Ticket category" (likely test/internal)
- Excludes any conversation tagged 'ignore-for-reporting' from Intercom
- Uses row_number() to deduplicate when conversations have multiple parts

PERFORMANCE CONSIDERATIONS:
- Distributed by conversation ID for optimal join performance
- Sorted by creation date for time-series queries
- Table format (not view) to avoid CASCADE issues with materialized views

USAGE EXAMPLES:
-- Get daily conversation metrics
SELECT 
  DATE(created_at) as date,
  COUNT(*) as total_conversations,
  AVG(time_to_admin_reply) as avg_response_time,
  AVG(CASE WHEN rating IS NOT NULL THEN rating END) as avg_csat
FROM view_cx_conversations
GROUP BY 1
ORDER BY 1 DESC;

-- Analyze inbox workload
SELECT 
  inbox_name,
  COUNT(*) as conversation_count,
  AVG(conversation_length) as avg_length,
  SUM(CASE WHEN is_closed THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)::FLOAT / COUNT(*) as close_rate
FROM view_cx_conversations
GROUP BY 1;

RELATED OBJECTS:
- view_intercom_conversations_with_topics: Message-level topic analysis
- Metabase dashboards: Primary consumer for support metrics

LAST MODIFIED: 2025-02-20
OWNER: Data Team
================================================================================
*/

-- Step 1: Drop the _new table if it exists (cleanup from any previous failed runs)
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS view_cx_conversations_new;

-- Step 2: Create the new table with fresh data
CREATE TABLE view_cx_conversations_new
DISTKEY(id)
SORTKEY(created_at)
AS
WITH domain_to_org AS (
  -- Map email domains to org_ids, excluding generic email providers
  -- Only include domains that map to exactly one org for accuracy
  SELECT 
    LOWER(SPLIT_PART(email, '@', 2)) as domain,
    organizationid as org_id,
    COUNT(*) as user_count
  FROM public.mysql_users
  WHERE email IS NOT NULL 
    AND email LIKE '%@%'
    AND organizationid IS NOT NULL
    -- Exclude generic email providers
    AND LOWER(SPLIT_PART(email, '@', 2)) NOT IN (
      'gmail.com', 'outlook.com', 'icloud.com', 'hotmail.com', 'yahoo.com',
      'proton.me', 'protonmail.com', 'me.com', 'pm.me', 'live.com',
      'qq.com', 'aol.com', '163.com', 'fastmail.com', 'mail.com',
      'yandex.ru', 'mail.ru', 'gmx.com', 'gmx.net', 'web.de',
      'tutanota.com', 'duck.com', 'msn.com', 'comcast.net', 'verizon.net'
    )
  GROUP BY 1, 2
  -- Use the domain with the most users if there are multiple orgs
  QUALIFY ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY domain ORDER BY user_count DESC) = 1
),
intercom_conversations_filtered AS (
  SELECT
    ic.id,
    TIMESTAMP 'epoch' + ic.created_at * INTERVAL '1 second' AS created_at,
    it.name AS inbox_name, 
    ia.name AS admin_name, 
    ic.statistics.time_to_admin_reply::INTEGER AS time_to_admin_reply,
    ic.statistics.time_to_first_close::INTEGER AS time_to_first_close,
    ic.state = 'closed' AS is_closed,
    -- Bot user ID 6649497 indicates auto-closed
    COALESCE(ic.statistics.last_closed_by_id::VARCHAR = '6649497', FALSE) AS was_auto_closed,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY ic.id) AS rn,
    -- Calculate conversation length including initial message
    (LEAST(1, SUM(CASE WHEN cp.part_type = 'assignment' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) OVER (PARTITION BY ic.id)) +
     SUM(CASE WHEN cp.part_type = 'comment' AND cp.author.type IN ('admin', 'user') THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) OVER (PARTITION BY ic.id) + 1)::FLOAT AS conversation_length,
    -- Admin message count
    (LEAST(1, SUM(CASE WHEN cp.part_type = 'assignment' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) OVER (PARTITION BY ic.id)) +
     SUM(CASE WHEN cp.part_type = 'comment' AND cp.author.type = 'admin' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) OVER (PARTITION BY ic.id))::FLOAT AS conversation_admin_length,
    -- User message count (including initial)
    (SUM(CASE WHEN cp.part_type = 'comment' AND cp.author.type = 'user' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) OVER (PARTITION BY ic.id) + 1)::FLOAT AS conversation_user_length,
    ic.conversation_rating.rating::INTEGER AS rating,
    -- Extract up to 6 tags
    ic.tags.tags[0].name::TEXT AS tag_1,
    ic.tags.tags[1].name::TEXT AS tag_2,
    ic.tags.tags[2].name::TEXT AS tag_3,
    ic.tags.tags[3].name::TEXT AS tag_4,
    ic.tags.tags[4].name::TEXT AS tag_5,
    ic.tags.tags[5].name::TEXT AS tag_6,
    -- Get email from first contact in the conversation
    cont.email AS customer_email,
    -- Get org_id from mysql_users (exact match) or domain mapping (fallback)
    COALESCE(
      mu.organizationid,  -- First try exact email match
      dto.org_id          -- Then try domain match
    ) AS org_id,
    -- Get stripe_id via org_id lookup
    mo.stripeid AS stripe_id
  FROM public.intercom_conversations ic
  LEFT JOIN public.intercom_teams it ON it.id = ic.team_assignee_id
  LEFT JOIN public.intercom_admins ia ON ia.id = ic.admin_assignee_id
  LEFT JOIN public.intercom_conversation_parts cp ON cp.conversation_id = ic.id
  -- Join to get email from first contact
  LEFT JOIN public.intercom_contacts cont ON cont.id = ic.contacts.contacts[0].id
  -- Join to mysql_users for exact email match
  LEFT JOIN public.mysql_users mu ON LOWER(mu.email) = LOWER(cont.email)
  -- Fallback join to domain mapping for company emails
  LEFT JOIN domain_to_org dto ON LOWER(SPLIT_PART(cont.email, '@', 2)) = dto.domain
  -- Join to get stripe customer ID
  LEFT JOIN public.mysql_organizations mo ON mo.id = COALESCE(mu.organizationid, dto.org_id)
  WHERE ic.custom_attributes.["Ticket category"] IS NULL
    -- Critical: Exclude imported Hiver conversations to avoid duplicates
    AND (ic.tags.tags[0].name::TEXT IS NULL OR ic.tags.tags[0].name::TEXT <> 'ignore-for-reporting')
    AND (ic.tags.tags[1].name::TEXT IS NULL OR ic.tags.tags[1].name::TEXT <> 'ignore-for-reporting')
    AND (ic.tags.tags[2].name::TEXT IS NULL OR ic.tags.tags[2].name::TEXT <> 'ignore-for-reporting')
    AND (ic.tags.tags[3].name::TEXT IS NULL OR ic.tags.tags[3].name::TEXT <> 'ignore-for-reporting')
    AND (ic.tags.tags[4].name::TEXT IS NULL OR ic.tags.tags[4].name::TEXT <> 'ignore-for-reporting')
    AND (ic.tags.tags[5].name::TEXT IS NULL OR ic.tags.tags[5].name::TEXT <> 'ignore-for-reporting')
)
-- Get Intercom conversations (excluding imported Hiver data)
SELECT 
  id::VARCHAR,
  created_at,
  inbox_name,
  admin_name,
  time_to_admin_reply,
  time_to_first_close,
  is_closed,
  was_auto_closed,
  conversation_length,
  conversation_admin_length,
  conversation_user_length,
  rating,
  tag_1,
  tag_2,
  tag_3,
  tag_4,
  tag_5,
  tag_6,
  'Intercom' AS original_source,
  customer_email,
  org_id,
  stripe_id
FROM intercom_conversations_filtered
WHERE rn = 1

UNION ALL

-- Add original Hiver conversations with preserved metadata
SELECT
  h.conversation_id::VARCHAR AS id,
  h.created_at::TIMESTAMP,
  CASE 
    WHEN h._ab_source_file_url = '245420_hiver/Anna_Rhatigan_l184d_new.csv' THEN 'Anna'
    WHEN h._ab_source_file_url = '191527_hiver/Priority_l184d_new.csv' THEN 'Priority'
    ELSE SPLIT_PART(SPLIT_PART(h._ab_source_file_url, '/', 2), '_', 1)
  END AS inbox_name,
  CASE WHEN h.first_responder = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE h.first_responder END AS admin_name, 
  CASE WHEN h.first_response_time__business_hours___seconds_ = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE h.first_response_time__business_hours___seconds_::FLOAT END AS time_to_admin_reply,
  CASE WHEN h.resolution_time__business_hours___seconds_ = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE h.resolution_time__business_hours___seconds_::FLOAT END AS time_to_first_close,
  CASE WHEN (h.resolved_at <> 'NA' AND h.resolved_at IS NOT NULL) OR (h.status = 'Closed' AND h.conversation_type <> 'Outbound') THEN TRUE ELSE FALSE END AS is_closed,
  FALSE AS was_auto_closed,
  h.total_emails_count::FLOAT AS conversation_length,
  h.emails_sent_count::FLOAT AS conversation_admin_length,
  h.total_emails_count::FLOAT - h.emails_sent_count::FLOAT AS conversation_user_length,
  CASE 
    WHEN h.csat_rating = 'Not satisfied' THEN 1 
    WHEN h.csat_rating = 'Okay' THEN 3 
    WHEN h.csat_rating = 'Satisfied' THEN 5 
    ELSE NULL 
  END AS rating,
  CASE WHEN h.tags = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE SPLIT_PART(h.tags, ',', 1) END AS tag_1,
  CASE WHEN h.tags = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE SPLIT_PART(h.tags, ',', 2) END AS tag_2,
  CASE WHEN h.tags = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE SPLIT_PART(h.tags, ',', 3) END AS tag_3,
  CASE WHEN h.tags = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE SPLIT_PART(h.tags, ',', 4) END AS tag_4,
  CASE WHEN h.tags = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE SPLIT_PART(h.tags, ',', 5) END AS tag_5,
  CASE WHEN h.tags = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE SPLIT_PART(h.tags, ',', 6) END AS tag_6,
  'Hiver' AS original_source,
  CASE WHEN h.mailbox_contact = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE h.mailbox_contact END AS customer_email,
  -- Get org_id from mysql_users (exact match) or domain mapping (fallback)
  COALESCE(
    mu.organizationid,  -- First try exact email match
    dto.org_id          -- Then try domain match
  ) AS org_id
FROM public.hiver_new_conversations h
-- Join to mysql_users for exact email match
LEFT JOIN public.mysql_users mu ON LOWER(mu.email) = LOWER(CASE WHEN h.mailbox_contact = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE h.mailbox_contact END)
-- Fallback join to domain mapping for company emails
LEFT JOIN domain_to_org dto ON LOWER(SPLIT_PART(CASE WHEN h.mailbox_contact = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE h.mailbox_contact END, '@', 2)) = dto.domain;

-- Step 3: Atomic swap - drop old and rename new
BEGIN;

DROP TABLE IF EXISTS view_cx_conversations;
ALTER TABLE view_cx_conversations_new RENAME TO view_cx_conversations;

COMMIT;

toolUseResult.originalFile:
/*
================================================================================
TABLE: view_cx_conversations
PURPOSE: Unified view of all customer conversations from both Intercom and Hiver
================================================================================

DESCRIPTION:
This table provides a complete view of customer conversations across both Intercom 
(current system) and Hiver (legacy system used until April 2025). When we migrated 
from Hiver to Intercom, conversations were imported but lost critical metadata. 
This view reconstructs the complete picture by:
- Excluding Hiver-imported conversations from Intercom (tagged 'ignore-for-reporting')
- Including original Hiver data with full metadata preserved
- Providing unified conversation-level metrics across both platforms

USE CASES:
- Historical reporting spanning both support platforms
- Customer service metrics and KPIs
- Response time and resolution analysis
- CSAT tracking across the platform transition
- Team and admin performance metrics

KEY COLUMNS:
- id: Conversation identifier
- created_at: When the conversation started
- inbox_name: Team/inbox assignment
- admin_name: Assigned support agent
- time_to_admin_reply: Response time in seconds
- time_to_first_close: Resolution time in seconds
- is_closed: Whether conversation is resolved
- was_auto_closed: If closed by automation (bot ID: 6649497)
- conversation_length: Total number of messages
- conversation_admin_length: Number of admin messages
- conversation_user_length: Number of customer messages
- rating: CSAT score (1=Not satisfied, 3=Okay, 5=Satisfied)
- tag_1 through tag_6: Conversation tags
- original_source: Source platform ('Intercom' or 'Hiver')
- customer_email: Email address of the customer (from first contact for Intercom, mailbox_contact for Hiver)
- org_id: Organization ID using two-tier lookup:
  1. Exact email match in mysql_users (highest priority)
  2. Domain-based match for company emails when exact match fails (excludes generic providers)

DATA SOURCES:
- public.intercom_conversations: Current support platform (April 2025 - present)
- public.intercom_conversation_parts: Message details for length calculations
- public.intercom_contacts: Contact email information
- public.hiver_new_conversations: Legacy platform data (through April 2025)
- public.intercom_teams: Team/inbox information
- public.intercom_admins: Support agent information
- public.mysql_users: User information for org_id lookup
- public.mysql_organizations: Organization details

DEDUPLICATION STRATEGY:
Hiver conversations were imported into Intercom in April 2025 but tagged with 
'ignore-for-reporting' to prevent double-counting. This view:
1. Filters OUT imported Hiver data from Intercom side
2. Includes original Hiver data with complete metadata
3. Uses UNION to combine both sources without duplicates

FILTERING LOGIC:
- Excludes conversations with custom attribute "Ticket category" (likely test/internal)
- Excludes any conversation tagged 'ignore-for-reporting' from Intercom
- Uses row_number() to deduplicate when conversations have multiple parts

PERFORMANCE CONSIDERATIONS:
- Distributed by conversation ID for optimal join performance
- Sorted by creation date for time-series queries
- Table format (not view) to avoid CASCADE issues with materialized views

USAGE EXAMPLES:
-- Get daily conversation metrics
SELECT 
  DATE(created_at) as date,
  COUNT(*) as total_conversations,
  AVG(time_to_admin_reply) as avg_response_time,
  AVG(CASE WHEN rating IS NOT NULL THEN rating END) as avg_csat
FROM view_cx_conversations
GROUP BY 1
ORDER BY 1 DESC;

-- Analyze inbox workload
SELECT 
  inbox_name,
  COUNT(*) as conversation_count,
  AVG(conversation_length) as avg_length,
  SUM(CASE WHEN is_closed THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)::FLOAT / COUNT(*) as close_rate
FROM view_cx_conversations
GROUP BY 1;

RELATED OBJECTS:
- view_intercom_conversations_with_topics: Message-level topic analysis
- Metabase dashboards: Primary consumer for support metrics

LAST MODIFIED: 2025-02-20
OWNER: Data Team
================================================================================
*/

-- Step 1: Drop the _new table if it exists (cleanup from any previous failed runs)
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS view_cx_conversations_new;

-- Step 2: Create the new table with fresh data
CREATE TABLE view_cx_conversations_new
DISTKEY(id)
SORTKEY(created_at)
AS
WITH domain_to_org AS (
  -- Map email domains to org_ids, excluding generic email providers
  -- Only include domains that map to exactly one org for accuracy
  SELECT 
    LOWER(SPLIT_PART(email, '@', 2)) as domain,
    organizationid as org_id,
    COUNT(*) as user_count
  FROM public.mysql_users
  WHERE email IS NOT NULL 
    AND email LIKE '%@%'
    AND organizationid IS NOT NULL
    -- Exclude generic email providers
    AND LOWER(SPLIT_PART(email, '@', 2)) NOT IN (
      'gmail.com', 'outlook.com', 'icloud.com', 'hotmail.com', 'yahoo.com',
      'proton.me', 'protonmail.com', 'me.com', 'pm.me', 'live.com',
      'qq.com', 'aol.com', '163.com', 'fastmail.com', 'mail.com',
      'yandex.ru', 'mail.ru', 'gmx.com', 'gmx.net', 'web.de',
      'tutanota.com', 'duck.com', 'msn.com', 'comcast.net', 'verizon.net'
    )
  GROUP BY 1, 2
  -- Use the domain with the most users if there are multiple orgs
  QUALIFY ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY domain ORDER BY user_count DESC) = 1
),
intercom_conversations_filtered AS (
  SELECT
    ic.id,
    TIMESTAMP 'epoch' + ic.created_at * INTERVAL '1 second' AS created_at,
    it.name AS inbox_name, 
    ia.name AS admin_name, 
    ic.statistics.time_to_admin_reply::INTEGER AS time_to_admin_reply,
    ic.statistics.time_to_first_close::INTEGER AS time_to_first_close,
    ic.state = 'closed' AS is_closed,
    -- Bot user ID 6649497 indicates auto-closed
    COALESCE(ic.statistics.last_closed_by_id::VARCHAR = '6649497', FALSE) AS was_auto_closed,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY ic.id) AS rn,
    -- Calculate conversation length including initial message
    (LEAST(1, SUM(CASE WHEN cp.part_type = 'assignment' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) OVER (PARTITION BY ic.id)) +
     SUM(CASE WHEN cp.part_type = 'comment' AND cp.author.type IN ('admin', 'user') THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) OVER (PARTITION BY ic.id) + 1)::FLOAT AS conversation_length,
    -- Admin message count
    (LEAST(1, SUM(CASE WHEN cp.part_type = 'assignment' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) OVER (PARTITION BY ic.id)) +
     SUM(CASE WHEN cp.part_type = 'comment' AND cp.author.type = 'admin' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) OVER (PARTITION BY ic.id))::FLOAT AS conversation_admin_length,
    -- User message count (including initial)
    (SUM(CASE WHEN cp.part_type = 'comment' AND cp.author.type = 'user' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) OVER (PARTITION BY ic.id) + 1)::FLOAT AS conversation_user_length,
    ic.conversation_rating.rating::INTEGER AS rating,
    -- Extract up to 6 tags
    ic.tags.tags[0].name::TEXT AS tag_1,
    ic.tags.tags[1].name::TEXT AS tag_2,
    ic.tags.tags[2].name::TEXT AS tag_3,
    ic.tags.tags[3].name::TEXT AS tag_4,
    ic.tags.tags[4].name::TEXT AS tag_5,
    ic.tags.tags[5].name::TEXT AS tag_6,
    -- Get email from first contact in the conversation
    cont.email AS customer_email,
    -- Get org_id from mysql_users (exact match) or domain mapping (fallback)
    COALESCE(
      mu.organizationid,  -- First try exact email match
      dto.org_id          -- Then try domain match
    ) AS org_id,
    -- Get stripe_id via org_id lookup
    mo.stripeid AS stripe_id
  FROM public.intercom_conversations ic
  LEFT JOIN public.intercom_teams it ON it.id = ic.team_assignee_id
  LEFT JOIN public.intercom_admins ia ON ia.id = ic.admin_assignee_id
  LEFT JOIN public.intercom_conversation_parts cp ON cp.conversation_id = ic.id
  -- Join to get email from first contact
  LEFT JOIN public.intercom_contacts cont ON cont.id = ic.contacts.contacts[0].id
  -- Join to mysql_users for exact email match
  LEFT JOIN public.mysql_users mu ON LOWER(mu.email) = LOWER(cont.email)
  -- Fallback join to domain mapping for company emails
  LEFT JOIN domain_to_org dto ON LOWER(SPLIT_PART(cont.email, '@', 2)) = dto.domain
  -- Join to get stripe customer ID
  LEFT JOIN public.mysql_organizations mo ON mo.id = COALESCE(mu.organizationid, dto.org_id)
  WHERE ic.custom_attributes.["Ticket category"] IS NULL
    -- Critical: Exclude imported Hiver conversations to avoid duplicates
    AND (ic.tags.tags[0].name::TEXT IS NULL OR ic.tags.tags[0].name::TEXT <> 'ignore-for-reporting')
    AND (ic.tags.tags[1].name::TEXT IS NULL OR ic.tags.tags[1].name::TEXT <> 'ignore-for-reporting')
    AND (ic.tags.tags[2].name::TEXT IS NULL OR ic.tags.tags[2].name::TEXT <> 'ignore-for-reporting')
    AND (ic.tags.tags[3].name::TEXT IS NULL OR ic.tags.tags[3].name::TEXT <> 'ignore-for-reporting')
    AND (ic.tags.tags[4].name::TEXT IS NULL OR ic.tags.tags[4].name::TEXT <> 'ignore-for-reporting')
    AND (ic.tags.tags[5].name::TEXT IS NULL OR ic.tags.tags[5].name::TEXT <> 'ignore-for-reporting')
)
-- Get Intercom conversations (excluding imported Hiver data)
SELECT 
  id::VARCHAR,
  created_at,
  inbox_name,
  admin_name,
  time_to_admin_reply,
  time_to_first_close,
  is_closed,
  was_auto_closed,
  conversation_length,
  conversation_admin_length,
  conversation_user_length,
  rating,
  tag_1,
  tag_2,
  tag_3,
  tag_4,
  tag_5,
  tag_6,
  'Intercom' AS original_source,
  customer_email,
  org_id,
  stripe_id
FROM intercom_conversations_filtered
WHERE rn = 1

UNION ALL

-- Add original Hiver conversations with preserved metadata
SELECT
  h.conversation_id::VARCHAR AS id,
  h.created_at::TIMESTAMP,
  CASE 
    WHEN h._ab_source_file_url = '245420_hiver/Anna_Rhatigan_l184d_new.csv' THEN 'Anna'
    WHEN h._ab_source_file_url = '191527_hiver/Priority_l184d_new.csv' THEN 'Priority'
    ELSE SPLIT_PART(SPLIT_PART(h._ab_source_file_url, '/', 2), '_', 1)
  END AS inbox_name,
  CASE WHEN h.first_responder = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE h.first_responder END AS admin_name, 
  CASE WHEN h.first_response_time__business_hours___seconds_ = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE h.first_response_time__business_hours___seconds_::FLOAT END AS time_to_admin_reply,
  CASE WHEN h.resolution_time__business_hours___seconds_ = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE h.resolution_time__business_hours___seconds_::FLOAT END AS time_to_first_close,
  CASE WHEN (h.resolved_at <> 'NA' AND h.resolved_at IS NOT NULL) OR (h.status = 'Closed' AND h.conversation_type <> 'Outbound') THEN TRUE ELSE FALSE END AS is_closed,
  FALSE AS was_auto_closed,
  h.total_emails_count::FLOAT AS conversation_length,
  h.emails_sent_count::FLOAT AS conversation_admin_length,
  h.total_emails_count::FLOAT - h.emails_sent_count::FLOAT AS conversation_user_length,
  CASE 
    WHEN h.csat_rating = 'Not satisfied' THEN 1 
    WHEN h.csat_rating = 'Okay' THEN 3 
    WHEN h.csat_rating = 'Satisfied' THEN 5 
    ELSE NULL 
  END AS rating,
  CASE WHEN h.tags = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE SPLIT_PART(h.tags, ',', 1) END AS tag_1,
  CASE WHEN h.tags = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE SPLIT_PART(h.tags, ',', 2) END AS tag_2,
  CASE WHEN h.tags = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE SPLIT_PART(h.tags, ',', 3) END AS tag_3,
  CASE WHEN h.tags = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE SPLIT_PART(h.tags, ',', 4) END AS tag_4,
  CASE WHEN h.tags = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE SPLIT_PART(h.tags, ',', 5) END AS tag_5,
  CASE WHEN h.tags = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE SPLIT_PART(h.tags, ',', 6) END AS tag_6,
  'Hiver' AS original_source,
  CASE WHEN h.mailbox_contact = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE h.mailbox_contact END AS customer_email,
  -- Get org_id from mysql_users (exact match) or domain mapping (fallback)
  COALESCE(
    mu.organizationid,  -- First try exact email match
    dto.org_id          -- Then try domain match
  ) AS org_id,
  -- Get stripe_id via org_id lookup
  mo.stripeid AS stripe_id
FROM public.hiver_new_conversations h
-- Join to mysql_users for exact email match
LEFT JOIN public.mysql_users mu ON LOWER(mu.email) = LOWER(CASE WHEN h.mailbox_contact = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE h.mailbox_contact END)
-- Fallback join to domain mapping for company emails
LEFT JOIN domain_to_org dto ON LOWER(SPLIT_PART(CASE WHEN h.mailbox_contact = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE h.mailbox_contact END, '@', 2)) = dto.domain;

-- Step 3: Atomic swap - drop old and rename new
BEGIN;

DROP TABLE IF EXISTS view_cx_conversations;
ALTER TABLE view_cx_conversations_new RENAME TO view_cx_conversations;

COMMIT;

SELECT
  -- All base conversation columns
  c.*,
  
  -- Topic enrichment for Intercom conversations
  -- Check if conversation has 1583-related tags
  CASE 
    WHEN c.tag_1 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
      OR c.tag_2 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
      OR c.tag_3 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')

content.text: Now let me update the cumulative metrics view to use the stripe_id directly from view_cx_conversations_enriched instead of doing the complex join:

input.old_string:
intercom_conversation_events AS (
    -- Track cumulative count of intercom conversations per customer
    -- Each conversation represents a customer support interaction
    -- Join path: view_cx_conversations_enriched.org_id → mysql_organizations.id → mysql_organizations.stripeid
    SELECT
        o.stripeid as customer_id,
        DATE_TRUNC('day', ice.created_at) as event_day,
        COUNT(*) OVER (
            PARTITION BY o.stripeid
            ORDER BY DATE_TRUNC('day', ice.created_at)
            ROWS UNBOUNDED PRECEDING
        ) as cumulative_intercom_conversations
    FROM view_cx_conversations_enriched ice
    INNER JOIN mysql_organizations o ON ice.org_id = o.id
    WHERE ice.org_id IS NOT NULL
      AND o.stripeid IS NOT NULL
),
input.new_string:
intercom_conversation_events AS (
    -- Track cumulative count of intercom conversations per customer
    -- Each conversation represents a customer support interaction
    SELECT
        stripe_id as customer_id,
        DATE_TRUNC('day', created_at) as event_day,
        COUNT(*) OVER (

            ROWS UNBOUNDED PRECEDING

    FROM view_cx_conversations_enriched
    WHERE stripe_id IS NOT NULL
